reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a Transport Operations Manager with 10+ years in employee fleet/commute logistics, vendor management, and facilities coordination. The JD demands an embedded software engineer with C programming, MISRA compliance, Autosar, RTOS, and ideally RADAR/ADAS expertise. There is zero overlap: the candidate has no software development background, no embedded systems experience, no C or any programming language mentioned, and no automotive domain knowledge whatsoever. Even the educational background — a Diploma in Computer Science from 2012 — does not demonstrate any embedded SW competency. Do not waste interview bandwidth on this profile; reject immediately.
strengths: OPERATIONS SCALE: Managed end-to-end employee transportation for 2000+ employees across major corporates including Microsoft India and Novartis, demonstrating high-volume operational management — irrelevant to this embedded SW role.

STAKEHOLDER MANAGEMENT: Consistently promoted at Microsoft from Executive to Deputy Manager over 7 years, evidencing strong performance and leadership in transport operations — again, entirely outside the required domain.

REPORTING & COORDINATION: Prepared MOR and QBR reports and managed vendor SLA compliance across multiple enterprise clients — strong in facilities, zero relevance to embedded automotive software development.
gaps: ZERO EMBEDDED SOFTWARE SKILLS: The candidate has no C programming, no MISRA, no Autosar, no RTOS, and no RADAR/ADAS knowledge — every single technical must-have for this Senior SW Developer role is completely absent. This is a total domain mismatch, not a gap that can be bridged.

WRONG DOMAIN — TRANSPORT LOGISTICS NOT AUTOMOTIVE SW: This candidate works in corporate employee transport management and facilities. The JD requires an automotive embedded software developer targeting radar systems. These are entirely different industries and disciplines with no transferable technical value.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated; candidate appears to have transitioned to a new role in May 2025 so no penalty applied.
JD requires In-office presence in Chennai; candidate is based in Hyderabad (Beeramguda address).
Candidate has 10+ years of total experience, well above the minimum threshold numerically, though entirely in the wrong domain.
JD specifies no gender preference ('Any'); no penalty applied.
JD has no college preference or exclusion specified; no penalty applied.

availability_reason: Candidate joined a new role in May 2025 and is currently employed; no notice period stated in the resume.
self_reported_unverified: Claim of managing transport for '2000+ employees' at both Microsoft and Novartis — scale is stated but no operational metrics, cost savings, or efficiency outcomes are provided to substantiate impact.
Promotion chain from Executive to Deputy Manager at Microsoft cited as performance-based — no specific KPIs, targets met, or recognition details provided to verify basis for promotion.
interview_flags: Probe the gap between Nov 2024 (left Northern Tools) and Feb 2025 (joined TJX) — short but unexplained.
Probe the very short tenure at TJX (Feb 2025 to May 2025, only 3 months) — reason for leaving so quickly.
Ask whether candidate has any awareness of why they applied for an embedded software developer role given their transport management background — possible application error.
Verify whether the Diploma in Computer Science involved any programming coursework — even foundational — to understand technical baseline.
Clarify relocation intent to Chennai given candidate is based in Hyderabad.
